reviews.review_text: [h2] Cool game. It's like GTA SA (specifically) and Yakuza had a child.  [/h2]

Fast paced cinematic storytelling, seamless action, engaging open world, iconic outfits, memorable soundtrack, and visually incredible even in 2026.

If you're a HK film nerd, this game is a love letter to you

Breaking rocks in the hot sun, i fought the law and the law won........

7.5/10 this game is wei too overlooked

It's been a minute since I played a relatively old game, but Sleeping Dogs shocked me! From an engaging story to a unique combat system with an immersive world full of subtle details that made Hong Kong feel alive.

The story might seem simple at first, an undercover cop infiltrates a gang to dismantle them, but character & narrative progression allows it to tell an emotional and relatable story. Although the ending was expected, it was a blast watching it unfold. There are two sides of outlaws, the obvious criminal side full of corruption, anger, resentment, and bloodlust, and the human side full of honor, loyalty, and sacrifice, the game portrays both masterfully without one undermining the other, which gave the story its depth and meaning.

One thing I appreciate in the gameplay was that Wie Shen(main protagonist) had two different types of missions, police cases and criminal favours(main story). Both were very distinct in terms of gameplay which led to unique experiences. The world itself feels alive with a variety of random events that you can engage with if you need a break. I often found myself exploring and interacting with the world around me to avoid reaching the end so soon.

As a platinum enjoyer I HATED it. Collectibles are pain and side content felt tedious eventually, I found the DLC boring as well but I was never a fan of DLCs anyway. This doesn't hurt the game in anyway as its completely optional.

I recommend this game if you're looking for an immersive story with decent character & narrative progression with unique gameplay and an interactive world. I had a blast!

One last thing, people who compare this game to GTAV are the same people who are comparing Drake to Michael Jackson rn.

Aside from a few fun gimmicks, its a fairly by the numbers open world crime game. The thing that makes it so special all of these years later is Hong Kong. For whatever reason, this setting is criminally under utilized in video games. 

Really deserved a sequel to perfect everything.

This is a great game and though some of the mechanics are getting dated in 2026 its still worth playing.  Very GTA esq but you play as an undercover cop infiltrating the triad.  I would play this again.

It's a really good game. Much like GTA. But doesn't feel like just another clone.
The atmosplhere and characters are very well made. And so is the story. Really good.

I'd say give it a try.

I got it on sale. And it was worth all of it.
really love the gameplay, the combat is fun to play, love the QTEs, love the little detail interaction and interesting story

Things i like: 
-Combat is so cool it doesn't need recognition
-car driving like u can really drift specially if u understand it after practice its butter smooth
-skins like outfits u will love that section with different perks
-storyline is so great like literally make u experience a real life situation of a undercover cop 
-graphics are still great specially the water have similar effect of rdr2 not same but similar 
-perfect match for gta + watch dogs and at the time it release it was the best 
-maybe miss many things which i like 

things i don't like
-bike handling is so pathetic and take 3 seconds to start and rev 
-camera angle is so hectic sometimes literally gives me headache like on stairs and vaulting in closed area where camera is close to you, and also when u look around in a vehicle and drive (maybe my motion sickness problem)
-no manually jumping option, bud can jump from exceptional places but cant even jump on a bump! can make u stuck if falls between something!
-car literally bounce stuck on minor bumps instead of suspension, at speed don't take inputs properly ya maybe realistic but then surroundings doesn't act realistic! (ignore)
-combat is great but parry sometimes doesn't recognize properly only batman is able to give perfect parry and dodge! just be sure use it with timing for me sometimes works 
-camera angle while fight too go in another direction
-too lengthy absurd collectibles sometimes under the tunnel but u will like where it is wasting time looking around! how to enter here its in front of me but half of my height there is a wall with fence  i cant jump!
- 100% achievement is way too absurd the achievements it mention
- repetitive task for no reason just to increase gameplay time  

conclusion
this is one of the best game no doubt as a open world game if i keep side quest aside its loving and can be experience once after any period ! like the city u will really see the beauty in the rain the roads and the reflections its so realistic! u can own apartments cars and do jobs but just keep aside some absurd side missions that's so pathetic! sorry but i was going to punch the screen in some missions but still i luv it!!

After 100% the game, it is a masterpiece for open world crime based city games, here are some of things that are special or unique about this game compared to other games I've played like GTA or Saints row, or even Watch dogs 2, or Mafia 

-It takes place in Hong Kong, a far cry from the usual settings of the USA or any western country setting that is common for open world crime city games, this is most noticeable in the road direction of the cars being different, so if you are sick of the regular American enviorment's play this game

-If you are aware of the kung foo movies of the 90s, or Jackie Chan fights, fighting in this game feels like you are playing a kung foo movie with the environmental fighting and all

-The game allows you to kidnap people by putting them in the trunk, and also to put people in the back of a police car, so if you are looking to randomly role play as a police or such, you have freedom to do so with this game

-You play as a police officer an, it allows you to easily spawn in police cars, although this game doesen't feature a minigame like in GTA 4 when you enter a police car and you hunt down criminals, you still have police side quests placed around the map 


For some issues or nitpicks I had

-The story for this game was quite short, I also could not scratch my head at the choices and the random hostility made by Wei Shen, especially near the end

-You often find yourself carrying guns in the story missions, but for the most part carrying guns in this game is like disposing of a soda can, you often find weapons really disposable, and its very hard to find yourself carrying weapons in regular open world, as you cannot buy any guns, however finishing the game lends you a pistol in your apartments. 

I think that this can be blamed because of the fact you lack a inventory system with Wei Shen, and it makes sense for the beginning because its focused on hand to hand combat, but near the end game you often find enemies with guns and pistols

-Traid XP is terribly annoying to 100%, I had to replay all the story missions twice to reach level 10, meanwhile it only took me 50% of the games progression to 100% the police XP

The dlcs are also quite short and also pretty mideocre, but they aren't long enough where they are an hassel to play through, and at least the dlcs (excluding the car dlc and zodiac island trials) were seperate saves compared to the main game

one of the best open world games i've played.
hong kong is a really great setting for a game and this proved that
solid 8.5/10

This game is  a masterpiece of undercover crime drama. The combat is visceral, the Hong Kong setting is incredibly atmospheric, and the story is so gripping that you’ll be completely hooked from start to finish. My only complaint is that the game is too short—I wanted the story to keep going! If you love open-world action games with a great narrative, this is a must-play.

The fighting is very fun and story was also nice. I like the Hong Kong city, it look very cool at night. Driving was little hard sometimes but still enjoyable. Wei Shen is awesome character and make the game feel real. I think more people should played this game because it is underrated alot.

I had this game on my radar for years, but I never got the chance to play it until now, in May 2026. I can confidently say that the game performs very well in all technical aspects and runs smoothly on the Steam Deck.

The game features an engaging main story with great characters, all set in a vibrant Hong Kong filled with numerous side missions.

I would recommend this game as a must-play for all fans of GTA and open-world games.

Honestly, Sleeping Dogs Definitive Edition is an absolute masterpiece that still puts newer AAA games to shame.
The story is what completely hooks you. It is a phenomenal, gritty undercover cop drama that actually makes you feel the tension of living a double life. The emotional twists hit hard, the pacing is fast, and the narrative has way more heart and substance than most modern open-world titles released today.
Combine that amazing story with brutal, Arkham-style martial arts combat and a neon-soaked Hong Kong that feels incredibly alive, and you get a game that is pure fun from start to finish. 
If you haven’t played it yet, you are seriously missing out on one of the most entertaining gaming experiences ever made.
Please someone make a sequel already! Imagine a modern day open world game set in Hong Kong where you can perform insane stunts and live like an action star!
I wouldnt say this game is a masterpiece, but its really good and i do recommend it as its a very solid game, 8.5/10

Great game only complains are the cars feel more like driving a ship than a car, gun combat is really bad, and guns look terrible. (thankfully guns aren't super duper prevelant except late game). Game has an excellent story line with great twists and drama 8/10

Ever watch one of John Woo's excellent movies like, say, Hard Boiled or A Better Tomorrow? Well, minus some of the gun fu aspects, this is a close approximation. This is an unappreciated gem of a game. Plenty to do beyond the main storyline. The duality of working for/against the law is a nice touch. Combat is fun and fairly fluid, especially as you level up. I'd put the driving up against any of the GTA games. Same for clothing . It's got definite replayability. Another game I played on console, but had to check out on Steam.  There are much worse ways to spend your time.

♥♥♥♥ this version the original is 100 times better, this is basically the same game except its laggy AF the camera sucks, 1 mission blocked me in with traffic for no dam reason and stole my cash, i knew this mission as ive played the original game wich is 1 of the most enjoyable martial arts games ive played I cant put in to words how much of a ♥♥♥♥ around this game is and should not be but it is nothing compared to the normal version, I am pissed off and let down pok gai baat!

in the 1st version the controls where close to arcade perfect, excellent beat em up and not a bad driver, Iam am uninstalling this ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ dog and playing the original and the best!

piece by piece I am less and less interested in playing games in general!

What a fun game! A shame there is no sequel... Super atmospheric, super dynamic, the story & side missions are just plain entertaining and the game is not artificially stretched to have more playtime.
highly recomend, combat feels really nice and quests are very fun
I wasn't expecting much from this game, but the combat is very fun and the story, while not amazing, keeps you hooked until the end. A pretty cool game.

Great Game, kinda underlooked I really want to see such GTA killer games to come again cause this game has better Gameplay, Storyline, mechanics. 
This game was released a year before Rockstar's GTA V. If we have the same level access to Internet, PC and gaming industry back then could've gone toe to toe with GTA V.

I came for the GTA-style open world.

I stayed because apparently every problem in Hong Kong can be solved with a flying kick.

Sleeping Dogs is one of those games that somehow slipped under the radar, but once you start playing, it's hard to put down. The story is surprisingly good, Wei Shen is a fantastic protagonist, and the combat feels much more satisfying than simply shooting everything that moves.

One minute you're an undercover cop trying to maintain your cover. The next minute you're singing karaoke, street racing, helping random citizens, and launching criminals into air conditioners.

The driving is fun, the city feels alive, and Hong Kong is still one of the most memorable open worlds in gaming. The Definitive Edition also includes all the DLC, which means even more chaos for your money.

Is it perfect? No.

Some driving physics can feel a little strange, and occasionally you'll wonder why everyone in the city wants to fight you. But honestly, that's part of the charm.

If you've ever wished GTA had better hand-to-hand combat, a stronger story, and more opportunities to throw people into conveniently placed environmental hazards, this game is for you.

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

10/10 pork buns consumed.

A man who never plays Sleeping Dogs is never a whole man.

Is this game still worth playing in 2026?

TLDR; Yes! If you enjoy an open world crime/cop type of open world gameplay mixed in with Hong Kong as a setting and martial arts and gun play that can be quite fun in areas. Definitely a game I would recommend if you want to have some fun exploring Hong Kong and using martial arts to beat up people in a variety of ways, while enjoying a decently fun story, albeit somewhat predictable.

[b] Would Buy: [/b] [u][i] On Sale/under $10 [/u][/i]

[b] Pros: [/b]

1. Melee combat is pretty fun and unlocking new move sets by collecting certain collectables further expands your move sets.

2. The story of Wei Shen having to balance his undercover work with being a cop is an interesting take and helps mixes the open world "modern setting" type of crime/cop gameplay. At times you can forget that Shen is supposed to be a police officer and I think that's part of the design. 

3. The side missions can be quite fun, especially when you get to do missions for a particular scientists that lets you unlock something really awesome you can show up in style with. [spoiler] A vehicle with guns, EMP, and speed to wreck anything in your path. [/spoiler]

4. The tournament DLC/Addon is quite fun as it was heavily inspired by enter the dragon and lets you compete against others in a very similar fashion with some challenging fights.

5. Music is on point and rolling around listening to good tunes or going to the Karaoke bars in the city to try Shen's/your skills at singing can leave some pretty hilarious moments depending on how well or poorly you do.

6. Nightmare in Northend as far as gameplay goes is pretty fun and a direct reference to [spoiler] Thriller from Michael Jackson /[spoiler]. The villain in this DLC is pretty funny and is recommended to play it after you beat the main story as it is packed with spoilers.

7. Voice talent from some big names do pretty good work with their respective characters, though some being cameos they still do their best to bring enough talent to make things believable. 

[b] Neutral: [/b]

1. Plenty of vehicles to choose from ranging from motorcycles to vans. However you will probable end up choosing a certain type or style of vehicle and just end up using that most of the game. I chose motorcycle myself as it was the easiest to get places with, but most missions will require a normal passenger vehicle/car.

2. Clothing has some nice boons when worn together, as there are a lot to choose from which is great. Outside of the ones that give you bonuses to certain aspects of Shen's abilities, would be good to have more to choose from since what we do get seem a little limited when it comes to style. (This is a personal preference thing though as I ended up just rocking the legendary outfits or bonus outfits most of the game).

[b] Cons: [/b]

1. Gunplay can be fun but quite repetitive and can take away some of the challenges with several fights unless you opt to handicap yourself or try to use other creative means to deal with your foes with different weapons. Gunplay uses a bullet time mechanic which definitely has its fun moments, especially early on and in car chases.

2. The Nightmare DLC has this really annoying jump scare sound that full blasts no matter how low you turn down your volume (outside of muting it completely) that the first couple times is "hah, you got me" but having to do all the main missions and side objectives having this sound effect can get old really quickly.

3. The characters Shen can end up dating is pretty empty as you go on one "date" with the person to unlock map markers on the map. Repeat this for a total of 4 times to unlock all map markers and then those people become obsolete and never really show up in game after their main introduction and subsequent "date" quest/objective.

4. Story can be pretty predictable in parts as unless this is your first introduction to this type of story, you can probably guess who the final boss will be after the first act has finished. 

5. If you're looking to play a game where you can see Hong Kong culture in an open world game that revolves around crime and undercover work. You'll have to look elsewhere since Hong Kong feels like what American movies stylize it as instead of really doing much with the culture itself. 

6. Years later, there are still some minor bugs, nothing game breaking as the only bugs I've had were enemies doing some interesting poses thinking they were still alive but were actually dead, or Shen was supposed to climb over/up something but instead ended up flying 100s of meters/feet in the air and then dying due to fall damage. Which is pretty funny but also can be annoying when you're trying to find collectables or in a mission.

7. Repetitive side missions can get stale after a time. Thankfully there are a limited amount of these, but outside of where you are doing said missions, a lot of the side content (minus the cases you can take on) can feel repetitive as it's the same objective just a different coat of paint. Favors especially.
Great beat-um-up, fun story, cheap and awesome.
A man who never eat pork bun, is never a whole man! I've loved this game ever since it was on the ps3 and the definitive edition makes it even better!

Poor me, I discovered this masterpiece way too late. Definitely recommended for anyone who enjoys story-driven open-world games with great combat and atmosphere.

The first thing that surprised me was the combat. Instead of relying only on shooting, the game focuses heavily on hand-to-hand fighting, and it feels incredibly satisfying. Every punch, counter, and environmental takedown feels impactful. It almost feels like playing through a martial arts action movie at times.

The story was another big surprise. Wei Shen is actually a well-written protagonist, and the undercover cop angle keeps the story interesting throughout the game. The characters feel memorable, and the emotional moments hit harder than I expected from an open-world game.

Hong Kong as a setting feels refreshing even today. The streets, markets, neon lights, music, and atmosphere make the world feel alive. Driving around the city at night genuinely has its own vibe. The game may be older now, but the environment still holds up really well.

Man, this game has been sitting in my library for about 7 years, and I have no idea why I never touched this beautiful-ass game. It's honestly one of the biggest mistakes in my gaming backlog. Don't let the graphics fool you, Sleeping Dogs is an absolute gem. The gameplay is insanely fun, the hand-to-hand combat feels brutal and satisfying, the driving is great, and the story kept me hooked from start to finish.

The characters are memorable, the world feels alive, and Hong Kong is such a refreshing setting compared to the usual open-world games. Wei Shen is a badass protagonist, and the undercover cop storyline creates some genuinely tense and emotional moments. You'll be kicking ass, street racing, causing chaos, and getting invested in the story all at the same time.

My only real complaint is the camera. The camera angles can feel a bit awkward at times, and the controller camera movement feels kind of weird and dated. It's actually okay-ish with a mouse, but on a controller it can feel a little off. Then again, this game came out back in 2012, so that's probably just a product of its era.
it might be an older generation game, but it's still one of the most fun open-world experiences I've played. You'll definitely have a blast with this one.
third time i have bought this game on a different platform since its release many years ago, one of the best underrated games out there. 10/10

As a wise man once said a man is not whole without a pork bun in his hand.
This game is truly something special with this engaging narrative and changing gameplay, the hand to hand combat is especially so good (it is obviously inspired from the Arkham games), even though it is a open world game, guns are only secondary as you soon realize just how fun the hand to hand combat is with the environmental takedown which make it more satisfying to pull off. The two dlcs are also really good, my only problem is that the stat achievements are a pain in the ass to get.

I'd rate my enjoyment of playing this game an 8.5/10, but achievement hunting brings it down to a 6.5/10, as some parts can be a bit boring.
The story and combat are both enjoyable, though I feel like the characters move on from tragic events quite quickly. That could also be because I rushed through some quests. When it comes to bugs, there are quite a few, but nothing game-breaking.
I got this game for around €5 at the time, and for 48 hours of gameplay, it was definitely worth it!
Amazing game, best ive ever played, good story thats emtional and draws you in, mainly did the triad missions but its an amazing game for sure recoment

played this back in 2012, this game was a masterpiece. Broke kids GTA fr.

10000/10



re: controls can be wonky i changed most keybinds and the camera can be glitchy and spin all around if you flick to hard. definitely can be overlooked tho bc its meant to be played on controller.

Sleeping Dogs sounds like it is a brutal hardcore adult video game title but the truth is, the game is made in such a way that it's serious but still a heck of a lot of fun. Reason being, missions don't feel like a grind, they are fun, enjoyable and feel like they are leading to better things, because they are. The storyline is entirely plausible and as you learn more about Wei and the main characters around you, you start to feel a bond between the fictional folk. If you can handle the brutality and swearing you should play this game!
Fun game, unpolished compared to GTA, but it has an unique vibe, i recommend it

Insane game. In search of gold I found diamond. This game was soo worth buying for a completionist like me, I still can't thank heavens enough for finding it at the right time when it was up at sale. Anyways, coming to the game, I see an enormous amount of overworld to explore, might take me 60 hours just to finish it let alone the main story! It also definitely inspired maps of multiple modern gacha games like Genshin Impact or Honkai Star Rail. The driving is also crazy good remonds me of my retro days with Grand thief auto pre V era. Amazing concept, too late to the party tho XD
runs well even on old pc, love the fighting style in the game. worth it to buy on sale.
Such a good ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ game. I still get the urge to go back and replay it, from time to time.

I slept on this game when it game out, I'm sure I would have enjoyed it. I recall playing Yakuza 4 like 2 years later and I was like, oh is this game like that. Well it isn't, it is more like GTA, though I would say it felt like a little mix of both still. I liked dragging guys around.

Sleeping Dogs: Definitive Edition is one of the most underrated open-world games ever made.

The hand-to-hand combat is absolutely brutal and satisfying, making every fight feel straight out of a martial arts movie. Instead of relying only on guns, the game makes melee combat actually fun and impactful.

Hong Kong feels alive with its neon streets, markets, music, and atmosphere. Driving through the city at night while listening to the radio is a vibe on its own.

Wei Shen is also a fantastic protagonist, and the undercover cop storyline keeps things interesting from start to finish. The game balances serious moments, action, and emotional scenes surprisingly well.

A perfect mix of GTA-style open world and martial arts action.

Deserved way more recognition than it got.
Best game for combat melee combat, with simple paced storyline with tons of other activities to do in the game. Amazin storyline and great gameplay. Loved this game.

this game is 12 years old and looks beautiful, nice gameplay so far. i liked the environment and still playing the story to give my full review later.
i got it last year and it might be old, but its gold.
this game good, but idk if i use ds5 controller the camera always move i think its bug. i use xbox controller and its very fine

A hidden gem of open-world action

Sleeping Dogs is one of those games that doesn’t always get the spotlight it deserves, yet once you dive in, it’s hard to put down. Set in a beautifully realized Hong Kong, the game blends gritty crime drama with fluid martial arts combat, high-octane driving, and a surprisingly heartfelt story.

Open world, Crime/Cop sim, styled game with Arkham-Kung-Fu style combat. I remember getting this game from a friend from school, on my xbox 360 in 2012 around when it first came out.and I loved the game but had to return it. Now over a decade later, just recently replayed in 2026 and I might be biased but it still holds up to date. Great combat, story, exploration, and graphics.

This game is fun, badass, and quite engaging! I don't like GTA because the gameplay feels very weak. This game has tight combat and shooting, minor skill trees, a massive world with varied (enough) mission types, and a interesting story. I got hooked pretty quick, definitly recommend as it goes on a ridiculous sale pretty regularly!

I really enjoyed the story. I liked that the gameplay focuses more on hand-to-hand combat rather than guns like in GTA. The cars feel a bit clumsy and awkward to drive at times, but overall it's a very good game. Worth buying when it's on sale.
bro this is very good games with good graphics even in my potato laptop it is one of the best open world game with good story this game is not boring i completed it 3 times and i never bored
Wish this one would get a sequel, very underrated game. It has a great story and great gameplay.

This game is an odd mix of GTA and Judgement ( from the Yakuza series).  The story is pretty interesting, the main character acts like your run of the mill early 00's playboy, and the gameplay is still pretty good if you like God Of War style combat.  The driving is okay but some of the distance to missions can be a bit nerve racking.   There is some jank and glitches here and there but overall still enjoyable.  The DLC content is pretty bland though.   If you like the early Saints Row games then I'd give this a shot.

A Hong Kong Triads story with GTA style gameplay and music, Batman Arkham series combat, NFSUnderground style car racing feel, a pretty decent story and some chicky babes, what's not to love. It's also less than a beer on special!
A true classic still holds up to this day. I would become an undercover cop just for a chance at a sequel
Cant believe I'd never played this game. So up my alley. Love it. Fun combat, cool exploration. Good game
the best open world game ever created and 9 times out of 10, it's on sale
The combat is insane, character development is so good, overall storyline's amazing.. what not...

Best game ive ever played.
Not even joking 
Gedagdigdagdaoh i've been married a long time ago
Great fighting style and very good gameplay even on my potato laptop

This is a classic case of a game being more than the sum of its parts. No single element is excellent: the melee combat is a shabby version of the Arkham Batman games, the cover shooting is sloppy, the driving/racing is janky, and the writing is cringy throughout (the Bechdel test curled up and died while playing the game). But somehow, when you zoom out and take in the game as a whole. it's completely absorbing. Getting to run around a virtual Hong Kong certainly goes a long way in that regard. It's a real shame that Square cancelled the sequel. Their executives' unrealistic sales expectations strike again.
What an underrated game. Getting all the achievements was worth it after 78 hours of playing it.
idk why this game not hype at indonesia, this game actually release before GTA V, and have a same style and mechanic
great gameplay and story

It's really good game and you have to remember this game is decades old, the graphics are still top. This shows that a good art design never dies

This game is unique and one of the goats of its time when it competed with the likes of GTA 4, assassin's creed II and other big open world centered games when that was the trend.

This game gets often compared to GTA and Yakuza and I'm sure they took inspiration from them BUTTT this game holds it's own weight.

The Story is one that for me got more and more interesting the longer it went on, The Double life and it's  influence on Wei, had you always thinking "Which side is he gonna choose when/if it comes to a tipping point".
I have to say I didn't expect the story to go the way it did, with Pendrew being in bed with the Sun on Yee and then double crossing them for his own gain.

but the story wasn't flawless, I wish we had more depth into the possible romance option with the women you meet in the early game and they just vanish from the story entirely

The combat system is a mix of the Batman Arkham series and a proper martial artist

Being a Fighter myself, I really enjoyed knowing that GSP helped creating the Combat system which uses a lot of real moves we use daily in training and in fights.

The actors are very good, It's very believable and the brutality of the Triad's is shown as matter of fact and you don't question it.

The Characters, The Storyline, The Graphics, The City, The Missions and much more, truly make this game unique and deserves the title of "A Classic" and having played this game 14 Years after it's release I had a lot of fun but also a lot of intrigue with this game, It asks moral questions all throughout the game.

this game is somewhere between a 8 or 9 out of 10 only because the story with the romance options didn't make much sense and was left unfinished therefore they made a mistake of showing something on screen which wasn't utilized or utilized properly

Would definitely recommend this if anyone is thinking about buying, This game only costs 3 euro on sale with the DLC's and its very worth it for that price.
Sleeping Dogs is a must play game with all the hand to hand combat, cool missions and open world experience. Definitely a good gaming experience.
Aged like a fine wine. The cinematic, storytelling, side activities, combat and the world are great! One of the good games not named GTA.

So many good memories from this game, played first by pirated in my workbook with regular crashes and low fps, and somehow i still loved this game. Now i played this again on my new pc with good fps and no crashes, still got the same feeling, game is soooo underated. Hope they make a sequel

I have always seen this game since I was a kid, But I didn’t have the chance to purchase it,😕 But now that I got the chance this game is good if It was released today it would still do amazing numbers; It’s a very fun game if you just blackout negativity and enjoy it.

Got this a log while ago forgot to leave a review.The game is good imo,its like ur playing thru an action packed Hong Kong police movie.

The pros
Good Story,and good voice acting and action that captures the essence of the old Hong Kong movies

Melee combat,unlike in GTA or GTA clones,this one does things a bit different,instead of guns u rely on martial arts and melee weapons in fights and its fun once u get it

Ghraphics is still freaking good.Hong Kong really feels live in too with the cars and peds.

A decent amount of vehicles to choose from.Each vehicle also has their own unique feeling


The Cons
There is a learning curve to the combat system,took me like a few days to really get the hang of it.Could be more if ur a slower learner.But it is fun once u get it

The AI in races are really frustrating at times u made a few mistakes and catch up and sometimes u cant even catch back up.Maybe its my skill issue but some races REALLY make me wanna eat my keyboard.

the boats is kinda bugged(?) i cant turn sometimes on the bigger boats i dunno why.But there arent many missions requiring those bigger boats so its fine

Later in story u can get hold up by enemies it says to press E to escape,now like me u mostlikely mash the hell out of the E button.But u need to tap it in a rythym to get free.For a moment i thought the game was unplayable coz of that but i figured it out somehow lol

Dunno if this is considered a con but the story progression is really linear.I know some dont like those(?)

I will recommend this game when u can get it on sale,at full price maybe if i can,sure.It is a good game with good story.I would say its a 8/10 personally
This game has an amazing amount of narrative and it is also very story driven making it a very enjoyable game.
They finally fixed the cloud saving for this game which sucks because now I have one less excuse to replay it from the beginning every year

On the shorter side for open world games like this, but the overall story is quite engaging. Combat is really similar to the arkham/mordor games yet somewhat bareboned early on but you can upgrade moves as you progress. Game feels like an almost 50:50 mix of yakuza and GTA (minus the goofy shenanigans) so if you are a fan of either give it a shot, Game is like 10+ years old right now and it goes on sale constantly

[h1]Masterpiece[/h1]
Rarely do I find a game that I genuinely do not want to end, but Sleeping Dogs was one of those rare cases.

I honestly have no idea how this game slipped under my radar for so long, especially since it had apparently been sitting in my library for years. After finally playing it, I can easily say I wish I had experienced it much sooner.

The story is fantastic, and I got surprisingly attached to the characters. The world, atmosphere, and graphics still hold up really well, and the combat is easily one of the highlights. The fighting feels smooth, responsive, and satisfying without being overly difficult or frustrating.

I did not enjoy the DLC content quite as much as the main game, but even with that, this was one hell of a ride from start to finish.

It is a real shame we never got Sleeping Dogs 2, because this game absolutely deserved a sequel. I am definitely late to the party, but if you are like me, and you're unsure whether to buy it, I would say grab it. Even at full price, it is worth every penny.

[h3]A MUST HAVE[/h3]
nostalgic game. whenever i play this game i remember the very good old days when  i used to play this game with my brothers

an amazing game with good music, action and overall enjoyability. at first the game was not something i was familiar with. i got started quite slow but when i cought the taste of it i could not stop playing.

I liked the combat, although I don't think I ever fully mastered the mechanics, the story was fairly interesting, but I never really connected with any of the characters, and the pacing felt a little off in places, meaning that I think some big story beats happened way too fast and out of nowhere, and the typical 'driving from waypoint to waypoint to start a story, then driving to a new location again' got a little tiring for me too.
But with that said, I still enjoyed my time with Sleeping Dogs, it was fun and a little silly, which I like in my games, and I completed it in about 20 hours, I did a little over half of the side content I think.

4 out of 5 stars from me! :)

Honestly, if you love games like Grand Theft Auto but want something that "breaks the mold" a bit, you need to check out Sleeping Dogs. It totally holds its own against earlier GTA's, but what makes it so cool is the setting and the story. You're an undercover cop infiltrating the Triads in Hong Kong, and the narrative is just fantastic. Instead of just driving around shooting everyone, you're mostly doing brutal, martial-arts style martial arts brawling that feels like you're in an old-school kung fu flick. Just a heads up though, the game is definitely showing its age a bit. The driving can feel pretty stiff, shooting guns is kind of janky, and the free-running and camera angles get pretty clunky at times. But look, if you can look past some of that old-school jank, the story and the vibes of the city make it 100% worth picking up, especially since you can usually get it dirt cheap on sale.
Played this game on Xbox years ago, got it on sale for PC a while back but would have paid full price today. Runs great and looks good.

Fun remake. Very limited on movements and choices for a game on the remaster's year release. Though, for back then to the original one.. real good, so thumbs up.
I guess, we all just wish there was a new one from old Hong Kong launching any time soon

Excellent game, fantastic story.

Great combat and side content never gets drawn out.

All DLC are very good and the wheels of fury car is way to much fun!

Enjoyable to 100% and honestly better than GTA 8/10.

I enjoyed the game as a good GTA clone. 

# Story
There are things that the game does better than GTA such as not filling the main story with so many filler quests, there are some but not as much as GTA. Story is short and fun to follow, nothing special but fun. 

# Gameplay
Gameplay is fun, driving didn't bother me, it has a close feeling to old Need for Speed games. Gun play is not a huge part of the game but additional slow time is making it more bearable. Using the Batman series combat system suits the game itself.

# Quest Design
As expected the side quests are basically the same and I finished most of it in order to level up the face level. If you don't want to max out everything, there is no need to touch those quests, which might ruin your experience. Sticking the main story and some case quests would be a better approach to this game. I must say the main quests are not that unique as well, sometimes it doesn't differ from each other but it is a short game so it wouldn't bother someone who is focusing on the main story.

# Overall
Game got the ideas from different games and put together very well. It is not a must to play but it is definitely worth a shot.
Really a good game. Very underrated. Story is very good. Gameplay is fun. DLCs are boring. Main story is great. Worth it in 2026

main story is incredible. an undercover cop with a gritty past and resentment towards triads is sucked into the very world he hates, only for a push and pull conflict within himself the moment closeness and family are philosophies instilled in these syndicates. side quests are a little repetitive, but the fighting aspect of the game makes up for it. the amount of fighting combos and environmental attacks are so crisp and satisfying. 

world is beautiful. lots to explore and benefits from exploring. a few bugs and glitches here and there, but it doesn't take away the experience of this game.

Just beat this game, And All I have to say is The fact no big steamer isn't talking about this game Is lowkey sad af. You can Run this game On A offices PC spec are very low, This game Is So Awesome and Interesting af. It Has Many fun mechanics and combat combos, they also Have outfits with many unique animations and abilitys that each outfit has.
One my Favorites outfits is The Agent 47 outfit.

10/10 (Highly Recommended) I am totally satisfied with this game and absolutely loved every single second of it! The undercover cop story in Hong Kong is incredibly gripping, emotional, and well-written.The combat is the absolute best part—smashing enemies into environmental hazards feels amazing and never gets old. Driving through the neon-lit streets at night while listening to the radio creates an unmatched atmosphere. If you want a great open-world game with fantastic martial arts action, do not skip this gem. A pork bun in hand, and you are good to go!
Years later, still one of the GOATs. A must play for any fan of martial arts, Hong Kong cinema, and/or cinematic action.

Completed the game at least four times across platforms - all time favorite. There are things i wish it didn't have and vice versa, but its a great play all around. good length main story with plenty of side content. I'm sure ill come back and play it again more through the years
A hidden gem, Sleeping Dogs is very unique compared to other games in its genre. The Hong Kong setting, brawler combat and memorable story all come together.
Held up very well for being a PS3 game, the driving and shooting can be a bit clunky, but the combat is like BUTTA, if you see this game on sale, Buy.

Pure classic!

Excellent game that feels like a cop movie, runs smoothly on modern PCs. There are some glitches like the main character teleporting randomly when grappling someone and the AI in car races that are clearly far back in your minimap suddenly catching up to you out of nowhere. At times the camera is difficult to control and will zoom onto a random pillar or inconvenient angle during fights. As an Asian myself, I liked the corniness of the dialogue and Hong Kong setting. The story is great and the world is super immersive to play in.
Despite having numerous bugs and one of the worst "driving" experience I've had in games i enjoy it very much and still return to it every couple of years

overall if u see this game on sale dont hesitate! buy it really worth it game...

Sleeping Dogs: Definitive Edition is still one of the best open-world action games out there. The hand-to-hand combat is unique compared to other sandbox games and never gets old. The undercover cop storyline kept me hooked till the end.

The world design, soundtrack, and atmosphere are excellent. Even after many years, it still feels polished and fun to play. Highly recommended for fans of story-driven action games.
